pre_assessment.planned_strategy: Use by(bit_vector) with requires for mask51 value to prove each bound per limb
stated_uncertainties: Whether array indexing works in by(bit_vector)

attempt_1.code: [5 assert by(bit_vector) blocks directly using limbs[i] & mask51 + limbs[j] >> 51]
attempt_1.error_message: Verus interpreter panicked: Expected the argument to array_view to already be an Interp(Array(_)). Got: FreeVar(VarIdent("limbs", VirParam)) — bitvector solver cannot handle symbolic array indexing

attempt_2.revision_description: Extracted array elements into local variables (let l0 = limbs[0]; etc.) before the by(bit_vector) blocks to avoid symbolic array indexing in the bitvector solver

post_assessment.stated_surprises: The bitvector solver cannot handle symbolic array indexing — need to extract elements into locals first. This is a Verus-specific gotcha.
post_assessment.alternate_strategy: Could use sub-lemmas (lemma_masked_lt_51, lemma_u64_shr_le) as seen in lemma_reduce_bound_2p

diagnosis.strategy_detail: by(bit_vector) was the right approach — just needed array element extraction
diagnosis.verus_detail: First attempt failed because by(bit_vector) cannot handle array indexing on symbolic arrays — needed let bindings
